howItWorks.intro: This uv exposure calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Burn time = Skin type base (minutes) ÷ UV Index
Protected time = Burn time × SPF × 0.5 (real-world factor)
Capped at 8 hours — reapply sunscreen every 2 hours regardless
UV Index 1-2: Low, 3-5: Moderate, 6-7: High, 8-10: Very High, 11+: Extreme
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What UV index is dangerous?
faq.answer: UV 1-2: Low risk. UV 3-5: Moderate, wear sunscreen. UV 6-7: High, reduce midday exposure. UV 8-10: Very High, avoid 10am-4pm sun. UV 11+: Extreme, stay in shade. Most US summer days reach UV 7-10.

faq.question: Does SPF 100 give twice the protection of SPF 50?
faq.answer: No. SPF 30 blocks 97% of UVB, SPF 50 blocks 98%, SPF 100 blocks 99%. The difference is minimal. More important: apply enough (1 oz for full body), reapply every 2 hours, and reapply after swimming/sweating.

faq.question: Can you get sunburned on cloudy days?
faq.answer: Yes. Up to 80% of UV rays penetrate clouds. You can also burn in the shade due to reflected UV from sand (25%), water (10%), concrete (12%), and snow (80%). UV exposure is cumulative throughout the day.

faq.question: Is the UV Exposure Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the UV Exposure Calculator?
sections.body: The UV Exposure Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your uv index, skin type, sunscreen spf, you get instant results including time to burn, protected time, uv category. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting time to burn right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your uv index and need to find the right time to burn. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.
